clean_text: No more nothing. Alhamdulillah. Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala tells us in he says subhanahu wa ta'ala regarding the people of the book the Jews the Christians those who were given the revelations of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala and then proceeded to ignore more much of that which was revealed to them and proceeded to divide amongst one another out of spite towards one another out of arrogance between one another. this happened and such differing should not have occurred. Why is that? He says subhanahu wa'ta'ala regarding the people of the book the Jews the Christians he said subhanahu wa'tala and the people of the book they did not divide up amongst each other and group up into different groups and factions. and u opinions that were fighting and contradicting one another in their understanding of the core principles of their revelation. They only did this after their messengers came to them, after their respective messengers came to them. However, this should never have happened. Why? Because what should have united them all underneath all of that was the basic tenants of their religion which was they were not commanded except that they worship Allah making sincere to him all of their worship and follow and adhere to their messengers. This is what they were commanded and that they establish salah and that they give zaka and that is the straight religion of Allah. That is the religion of Allah. What does that mean? Every prophet and messenger came with that core message. If we were to have a devout follower of Musam amongst us today or Isa or NIM or L or H or or any one of these Muslims from those past nations, if they were amongst us today, the details of their worship will be different than ours. The details will be different. However, the core tenants would be exactly the same. There'd be no difference between us and them in the core tenants of the religion. Meaning we would all agree on the worship of Allah alone without any partners, making sincere to him all acts of worship and that we should only worship in the way that our respective prophets taught us. That is the religion of Allah from the time of Adam until the last human being on this earth. This is what we were created for. There was never any deviation from that and there was never any confusion in that from the messengers and the prophets and the scholars. The confusion came when people started to twist and play with the religion of Allah for worldly gain or personal gain or because their mind had become warped by presuppositions presupposing something and then looking for evidence for that thing in the Quran and Sunnah. This is what we refer to and we call innovation. That's what innovation is. And so when these groups, when these nations, these followers of the prophets started to fight with each other and started to compete with each other. What did they start to do? They started to contradict each other for the mere point of contradicting one another. And for this reason, Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala said regarding those who differ in their religion, listen to this verse. He says, he says indeed those who have differed and divided in their religion. You are not of them in any way. He was speaking to the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. Indeed those who differ in their religion and contradict one another and split up into factions in groups you oh Muhammad have nothing to do with them. Nothing to do with them in this life and in the hereafter. Nothing to do with them. Meaning all of them will claim that Muhammad sallall.aii wasallam is their prophet and they follow him. However, it's not about claiming to be the follower of Muhammad sallall.aii. It's about if Muhammad claims you. This is what matters. It's not about if you claim to love Allah. Everybody claims to love Allah. That's not unique at all. Every human being claims that. Speak to anyone who believes in Allah and has not learned their belief from from Allah. No matter how wicked or evil they are, they will tell you, "I'm sure that God is happy with me. I'm sure that he's fine with me. Right? You look you you look even to disbelievers, people that worship other than Allah, atheists, people who disbelieve in Allah, they claim paradise for each other always say, "Well, you know, God rest his soul. I'm sure he's in a better place now." How do you know that? What did this person do to find himself in a better place, right? And so the claim that you love Allah, everybody claims that. What is the notable thing is if Allah claims to love you, if he loves you, Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala says, to them to your followers, tell them if you all claim to love Allah, then follow me and Allah will in turn love you. to lay claim to the love of Allah. This is not unique. It's whether Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala loves you back. To lay claim to the prophet Muhammad sallall.aii wasallam is not unique. Every single person does that. It's whether he lays claim to you in this life and in the hereafter. That is what matters. Everything else falls to the wayside. So when we look at this verse, Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala tells us that these people of the book, they differed with each other. And you, oh Muhammad, you are not of them in any way. And those who follow you, but differ with one another in the essential core values of the religion of Islam. You are not of them in any way. He says subhanahua. The first one indeed those who divide up their religion. And the other recitation indeed those who depart from their religion. What does that mean? And so we understand from this that the dividing up of the religion comes as a result of the of the departure of mankind from the religion of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. And we're not speaking about differences of opinion on issues regarding issues of issues that the scholars of Islam differ on. That is considered to be of interpretation. We're not speaking about that. Introducing newly invented matters, innovation, these are things that are not open for discussion and they are not unclear which is an oxymor. It's a double negative. summary: This lecture discusses how religious divisions arise among followers of faith, particularly addressing the Quranic verse about those who divide their religion into factions. The speaker explains that the core message of all prophets—worshipping Allah alone, establishing prayer, and giving zakat—should unite believers, but divisions occur when people innovate and deviate from the essential tenants of their faith. The speaker emphasizes that claiming to follow a prophet or love Allah is not unique; what matters is whether Allah claims you and whether the Prophet Muhammad recognizes you as his true follower. The lecture distinguishes between scholarly differences of opinion on interpretive matters and fundamental divisions on core Islamic beliefs and practices.
suggested_title: Unity in Core Islamic Beliefs: Understanding Division and Innovation in Religion
catchy_title: Why Do Muslims Divide Over Islam's Core?
